free_text: The reaction Ni-58(n,p)Co-58 at different neutron
energies
6 MeV van de Graaff, and-
1.3 MeV Cockroft-Walton.
Deuterium gas target, or-
zirconium-tritide target.
Fluxes and energy spreads were as follows-
at 8.4 MeV, flux 1.5 x 10**8 n/sr/sec in the forward
direction, energy spread 200 keV.
At 14.1 MeV, flux 10**8 n/sr/sec at 90 degrees
energy spread 200 keV.
At 22 MeV, flux 0.20 x 10**8 n/sr/sec in the forward
direction, energy spread 300 keV.
Ni-58 enriched to 99%, thickness
9.6 mg/cm2 at 8.4 MeV, 30 mg/cm2 at the other energies.
Direct detection of emitted particles.
Counter telescope consisting of
two gas scintillators as delta-E counters, and a
CsI(Tl) crystal as total energy detector. Proton
telescope consisting of polythene radiator and
CsI(Tl) crystal to monitor the flux.
Li6-I(Eu) scintillator also used to measure
the neutron flux.
detector description
Measured by replacing nickel
target by polythene, normalization value not given.
Laboratory energy scale corrected for energy losses
in the delta-E detectors and target and transformed
to centre of mass. Counts in each channel adjusted
to give spectrum for equal intervals of the centre
of mass scale.
No information about source of uncertainties
Most data from NEUDADA, some taken directly from
the publication.
DATA-ERR heading in Subent 007 corrected.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
BIB updated. Corrections in all Subents.
data given on figs 1,3,5 of Nucl.Phys.,70(1965)89
SD: Value at Ecm =7.02 MeV (En=8.4 MeV and
ANG=60.degr.) corrected (89. -> 8.9 mb/sr/MeV) COS->ANG
DATA -> DATA-CM. SF3=P -> X; SF4=Co-58 -> H-1 in
REACTION code.
Data probably obtained by integration of measured
double differential cross-section in given ranges
of residual nucleus excitation energy. However,
data is given at some angles for which no double-
differential cross section exists in the NEUDADA
file and is not present in this work.
22 MeV values are averages over 2 or 3 runs.
data given on figs 2,4,6 of Nucl.Phys.,70(1965)89
SD: COS -> ANG; DATA -> DATA-CM. EN-RSL
corrected at En=22.MeV (3.0 -> 0.3 MeV).
Found from gradient of statistical plot of the
measured proton energy spectra from Ni-58(n,p),
using the inverse cross-sections (see REL-REF).
Data for 22 MeV believed to be inaccurate due to
influence of direct effects.
